referenceDocuments.name: NSP
referenceDocuments.description: Network Strategy Plan 2025-2029

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1
referenceDocuments.description: Manage network performance through 'Network-minded' decision-making

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/1
referenceDocuments.description: Operationally performant network and ATM/CNS services, established locally and/or network-wide

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/2
referenceDocuments.description: Improved predictability

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/3
referenceDocuments.description: Network performance cockpit (as part of iNM)

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/4
referenceDocuments.description: Continue to foster operational behaviours towards network performance

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/5
referenceDocuments.description: Strengthen operational stakeholders' performance planning

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 1/6
referenceDocuments.description: Further strengthen the close coordination of the whole cycle of technological modernisation for the execution of network functions

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2
referenceDocuments.description: Digitalise aviation through the deployment and integration of interoperable and secure information management systems

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/1
referenceDocuments.description: Continue to implement the Network Interoperability Strategy

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/2
referenceDocuments.description: Developing technical infrastructure and tools to support network performance cockpit

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/3
referenceDocuments.description: Modernise the NM systems architecture

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/4
referenceDocuments.description: Support to the evolution of interoperability standards and their coordination at ICAO level

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/5
referenceDocuments.description: Aviation Network Information Reference

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 2/6
referenceDocuments.description: Implement European data management systems and aeronautical information relevant to the execution of network functions as well as an electronic integrated briefing portal (within iNM scope)

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3
referenceDocuments.description: Sustainable and optimised Network design

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/1
referenceDocuments.description: Optimum network, responding to SES performance targets

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/2
referenceDocuments.description: Further enhance NOP planning process to include network optimisation actions

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/3
referenceDocuments.description: Further cross-border free route airspace implementation, in line with the ERNIP, Further TMA optimisation

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/4
referenceDocuments.description: Further Advanced FUA implementation

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/5
referenceDocuments.description: Optimum opening schemes

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/6
referenceDocuments.description: Operational Excellence

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 3/7
referenceDocuments.description: Network integration of new entrants

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4
referenceDocuments.description: Sustainable and optimised Network operations

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/1
referenceDocuments.description: Modernise local/network ATM system capabilities

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/2
referenceDocuments.description: Improve the quality of the European Aviation Network Planning Processes; Improve coordination of ATM transition projects

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/3
referenceDocuments.description: Collaborative Traffic & Network Management; Transformation of DCB into a collaborative constraint management process; Ensure the management of an enhanced Demand Capacity Balancing process

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/4
referenceDocuments.description: Implementation and coordination of end-to-end 4D Trajectories; EPP Conformance Monitoring; Integration of VFR and iOAT Flight planning; Evolve NM's role to become the planner and coordinator of the 4D Business/Mission Trajectory

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/5
referenceDocuments.description: Integration of all major European airports into Network Operations

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/6
referenceDocuments.description: Improved network situational awareness

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 4/7
referenceDocuments.description: Monitor airspace user service improvements

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5
referenceDocuments.description: Aim to provide on-time operations for and at airport platforms with the support of the Network Manager

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/1
referenceDocuments.description: On-time execution of schedules

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/2
referenceDocuments.description: Extend NOP planning function to include the monitoring and identification of potential improvement measures for better on time execution of the airline schedules

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/3
referenceDocuments.description: Support the operations at and around airport platforms with appropriate data

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/4
referenceDocuments.description: Enhanced exchange of information between NMOC and APOC

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 5/5
referenceDocuments.description: Inter-modal connectivity

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6
referenceDocuments.description: Ensure network resilience, safety and security and reinforce crisis management

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/1
referenceDocuments.description: Identification of major safety risks from a Network perspective

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/2
referenceDocuments.description: Safety will be transparent and expressed in terms of actual operational risks

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/3
referenceDocuments.description: Cybersecurity

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/4
referenceDocuments.description: Implement security standards

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/5
referenceDocuments.description: Enhance crisis management and the EACCC

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 6/6
referenceDocuments.description: Resilience and upwards/downwards scalability and societal needs

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7
referenceDocuments.description: Optimise and Digitalise (as appropriate) ATM/CNS infrastructure and services to support evolutions towards more efficient network operations and services

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/1
referenceDocuments.description: Optimised and effective use of the aeronautical radio frequencies

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/2
referenceDocuments.description: Robust and transparent Radar Transponder Code Function, including the SSR code allocation process

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/3
referenceDocuments.description: Monitoring and reporting of performance and real-time service status

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/4
referenceDocuments.description: Propose and facilitate modernisation and decarbonisation of the ATM/CNS infrastructure

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 7/5
referenceDocuments.description: Ensure the development and provision of common network support services

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 8
referenceDocuments.description: Develop the network people and improve its flexibility through excellence

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 8/1
referenceDocuments.description: Flexible ATS provision arrangements

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 8/2
referenceDocuments.description: Functional mobility of Air Traffic Control staff, harmonise ATCO's employment requirements

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 8/3
referenceDocuments.description: Performance indicators to monitor human factors and their impact on network performance

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 9
referenceDocuments.description: Towards net zero emissions aviation

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 9/1
referenceDocuments.description: Optimisation of flown trajectories

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 9/2
referenceDocuments.description: Greener aviation in the areas of fuel consumption, gas emissions, noise reduction

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 9/3
referenceDocuments.description: Operational and technical support to innovative concepts of operations and experimentation of new air transport vehicles

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 9/4
referenceDocuments.description: Network Manager capabilities to support the EC's environment policy

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 10
referenceDocuments.description: Support European aviation at global level

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 10/1
referenceDocuments.description: Network of Networks

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 10/2
referenceDocuments.description: Collaborative partnerships

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 10/3
referenceDocuments.description: Operational cooperation beyond Europe

referenceDocuments.name: NSP SO 10/4
referenceDocuments.description: Implementation of common ATM standards

stakeholderCategories.name: APT Unit
stakeholderCategories.description: Airport Integration Team within Network Manager

stakeholderCategories.name: Airport Domain
stakeholderCategories.description: Airport Domain within Network Manager (APT Unit)

stakeholderCategories.name: WOC
stakeholderCategories.description: Weather Operations Centre within Network Manager

stakeholderCategories.name: TCF
stakeholderCategories.description: Network Manager Transponder Code Function Team

stakeholderCategories.name: AMC
stakeholderCategories.description: Airspace Management Cell - manages airspace structures and restrictions

stakeholderCategories.name: ATC Unit
stakeholderCategories.description: Air Traffic Control Unit (includes civil and military ATC)

stakeholderCategories.name: TWR
stakeholderCategories.description: Airport Tower (includes TWR ATCO, TWR SUP)

stakeholderCategories.name: Airspace User
stakeholderCategories.description: Airspace Users are entities that utilize airspace for their operations, including commercial airlines, general aviation, military, and other operators

stakeholderCategories.name: CFSP
stakeholderCategories.description: Computerized Flight Service Provider - provides flight planning and related services on behalf of airspace users

stakeholderCategories.name: National Authority
stakeholderCategories.description: National Aviation Authority / Civil Aviation Authority (CAA)

stakeholderCategories.name: System Integrator
stakeholderCategories.description: Organizations integrating ATM systems and services

stakeholderCategories.name: Ground Handling Agent
stakeholderCategories.description: Ground handling service providers at airports

stakeholderCategories.name: Third Party Supplier
stakeholderCategories.description: Third-party service providers (refuelling, catering, PRM services, etc.)

stakeholderCategories.name: Surveillance Data Provider
stakeholderCategories.description: Providers of surveillance data for aircraft tracking

stakeholderCategories.name: EANPG
stakeholderCategories.description: ICAO European Air Navigation Planning Group
